mission.title: keycloak: Remove "You are already logged-in" during authentication
mission.description: When a user is already authenticated in one browser tab and initiates a new authentication flow in another tab, Keycloak displays a confusing "You are already logged-in" message. This degrades the user experience, especially when using multiple applications with the same Keycloak realm. Requested by 22+ users.

steps.title: Check current Keycloak setup
steps.description:
Verify your Keycloak version:
```bash
# If running in a container:
docker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh show-config

# Or check the container image version:
docker inspect keycloak | grep -i image
```
This fix is available in newer Keycloak versions. See [PR #23517](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/23517) for the version that includes it.

steps.title: Understand the issue
steps.description:
In previous Keycloak versions, when a user authenticates in one browser tab (tab1), the entire `RootAuthenticationSessionModel` is removed from the `authenticationSessions` cache. If the user has a pending authentication in another tab (tab2), that session is lost and the user sees "You are already logged-in" instead of being silently authenticated or redirected.

This is a long-standing issue originally tracked in [KEYCLOAK-5179](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-5179).

steps.title: Upgrade to the fixed version
steps.description:
Upgrade your Keycloak installation to a version that includes the fix from [PR #23517](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/23517):
```bash
# Example: upgrade the Keycloak container
docker pull quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest
docker stop keycloak
docker rm keycloak
docker run -d --name keycloak \
  -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin \
  -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin \
  -p 8080:8080 \
  quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest start-dev
```
The fix modifies how authentication sessions are handled so that concurrent tabs do not interfere with each other.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test the fix by opening multiple browser tabs:
1. Open tab1 and navigate to an application protected by Keycloak
2. Open tab2 and navigate to a different (or the same) application
3. Log in via tab1
4. Return to tab2 and initiate login
5. Confirm that tab2 no longer shows "You are already logged-in" and instead proceeds with authentication smoothly

resolution.summary: The fix modifies Keycloak's authentication session handling so that completing authentication in one browser tab no longer destroys pending authentication sessions in other tabs. This eliminates the confusing "You are already logged-in" message and allows concurrent authentication flows to complete independently.

prerequisites.description: A running Keycloak server (via Docker/Podman or Kubernetes) with Admin Console access.
